message: Hey, kind of need this sorted soon-ish, my exam is tomorrow morning and I still can't access the portal
notes: understated tone ('kind of', 'soon-ish'), no urgent keyword or exclamation, but a real next-day deadline plus a blocker -- confirmed high, not urgent: urgent means same-hour time pressure, a next-morning deadline is high even with an active blocker

message: Could someone please look at this when you get a chance? My exam starts in an hour and the portal won't load.
notes: polite phrasing ('when you get a chance') directly contradicts the actual deadline -- exam in an hour is close enough to the spec's own 'exam is starting' urgent example that the polite wrapper shouldn't downgrade it

message: Oh wonderful, the video's buffering again. Third time this week. Really enjoying this experience.
notes: sarcasm masking real frustration about a recurring blocking issue -- proposed high (clear complaint/frustration + blocking), not urgent, since there's no time-critical-right-now element

message: The live class video call isn't connecting for anyone in today's session, the whole batch is stuck outside.
notes: flat, calm tone with zero exclamation marks or urgency keywords, but describes a session happening right now with broad impact -- content should override tone here

conversation.body: Hi, I have a small doubt about DP
actually nvm, more urgent -- my live session link isn't opening and it starts in 3 minutes

notes: urgency only becomes clear in the second message -- tests the same context-awareness the category fix relies on, but for priority; near-exact match to the spec's own 'class starts in 5 minutes' urgent example

conversation.body: Hey, my payment page is stuck loading
It's been like this for 20 minutes
The course session I paid for starts in 15 minutes and I still can't confirm the payment went through

notes: escalating multi-turn with no 'urgent' keyword anywhere -- urgency has to be inferred from the 15-minute deadline stated flatly in the third message; lowest-confidence case in this batch, could plausibly be argued as high instead since the blocker is nominally a payment confirmation rather than the session access itself

conversation.body: just letting you know the certificate download link seems broken
also just realized today is the last day to submit for placement documentation and the link still isn't working

notes: same-day hard deadline stated calmly -- confirmed high, not urgent: same-day deadlines with an active blocker are explicitly high per the tightened priority definitions, even when calmly worded

message: No rush at all, just wondering if there's a live session about DSA sometime in the coming weeks.
notes: negative control -- explicitly low-urgency language paired with session-related content, to check the classifier isn't just pattern-matching on category keywords and over-calling urgency across the board
